== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]

== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.

The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.

The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.

Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.

Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.

Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0217.html 田上大水神社（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0218.html 田上大水御前神社（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]

== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.

The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.

Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. == Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "豊受大神宮}}" to "豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.

The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

== 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0217.html 田上大水神社（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0218.html 田上大水御前神社（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}

== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}

== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanoe Omizu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]]
== Overview ==
{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the **left** side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces **south**, while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces **east**<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "**Maruyama-san**" and "**Kurumazuka**" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient burial mound called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Burial Mound No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the burial mound has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>.
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Japan]] [[ja:田上大水神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} is **Ogoto Kannushi** (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} is **Miyako** (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine**: Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine**: Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * **Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine** enshrines **Kojin no Kanushi** (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * **Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine** enshrines **Miyako** (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional **[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]** architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as **Maruyama-san** (丸山さん) and **Kurumazuka** (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== location == * **Address:** 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * **coordinates:** {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]

{{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}<ref name="ic"/>.
