revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.

They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.

The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.

The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}

As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
