He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "伊勢市の神社" to "Shrines in Ise City"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|12=draft|12=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|12=draft|12=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|12=draft|12=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|12=draft|12=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|12=draft|12=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|12=draft|12=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.

Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|12=draft|12=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:田上大水神社 up to rev 95751676
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 |画像= [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]藤里町字大丸679 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 50.2 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 26.7 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田上大水神社：小事神主
田上大水御前神社：宮子 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 丸山さん、車塚 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''田上大水神社'''（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。外宮の摂社16社のうち第5位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11 - 12ページ。本記事では田上大水神社と同じ社地にあり、外宮摂社のうち第6位である'''田上大水御前神社'''（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）についても記述する。 両社の[[祭神]]は、外宮[[神官]]家の祖先であり、[[父子]]であるとされる伊勢文化舎（2008）：38ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]藤里町に鎮座する。外宮宮域・[[高倉山 (伊勢市)|高倉山]]に鎮座する[[山末神社]]の南に位置する。周辺は[[宅地]]化が進んでいるが、かつては[[水田]]が広がり、神社[[境内]]の[[森林|森]]は現今より目立っていた。付近には宮崎氏神社跡や山宮祭場跡など[[度会氏]]にまつわる[[史跡]]がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：268ページ。鎮座地の[[丘]]は[[濠]]に囲まれ学研パブリッシング（2013）：59ページ、東西22[[メートル|m]]、南北43m、比高5.5mである。宮域[[面積]]は約1,133[[平方メートル|m2]]。 田上大水神社と田上大水御前神社の[[社殿]]は同じ[[玉垣]]の中に鎮座する。社殿正面に向って左側面に鎮座するのが田上大水御前神社である式内社研究会 編（1990）：270ページ。社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、田上大水神社は南向き、田上大水御前神社は東向きに建てられている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。周辺住民からは「丸山さん」、「車塚」と呼ばれ、親しまれる。社地は「車塚一号墳」という名の[[古墳]]であり、[[前方後円墳]]だとされる。ただし、古墳は原形をとどめていない。[[牛車|御所車]]に形が似ていることから「車塚」と呼ばれる。 社名が長いことや、[[草奈伎神社|大間国生神社]]が「大間社」と「国生社」の2所からなることから、「田上」と「大水」の2所からなるとする[[古書]]（『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』など）もあるが、これは誤りである式内社研究会 編（1990）：267ページ。 == 祭神 == 田上大水神社の祭神は小事神主（おごとかんぬし）。外宮の[[世襲]]神主であった度会氏の祖先であるとされる。『[[度会県|度会縣]]宮社検査録』などの[[明治]]期の文献では祭神を不詳とするものが多い。 田上大水御前神社の祭神は、小事神主の[[娘]]であり、[[斎宮|斎王]]に奉仕したとされる宮子（みやこ）。 社名から推測できるように、元は[[水神|水の神]]であったのではないかとされる。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された。また、『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。社名は『延喜式神名帳』では「田上大水神社」、『止由気宮儀式帳』では「田上神社」と記す。 [[中世]]の末には[[祭祀]]が行われなくなる式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ。[[権禰宜]]の出口延良（度会延良）が[[承応]]元年（[[1652年]]）に度会四門の氏人らとともに再建した。この後の[[大宮司]]・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）による他の摂社の再興事業は、田上大水神社の再興に大きな影響を与えた。こうした再興の経緯により、[[明治]]初期の神宮改正まで氏人によって社殿の造り替えが続けられた。 社殿は[[1923年]]（大正12年）3月に建て替えられている宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ。また[[1958年]]（昭和33年）12月にも建て替えが行われ、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[9月20日]]に大修繕が施された。 == 交通 == * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「庁舎前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩約3分（約300m）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北上、「県庁舎前」[[交差点]]を左折、約2分（約1[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 ;山末神社から * 神社の入り口から南下し、木材置き場の前の交差点を左折、その先の園芸店（[[クラギ|農業屋]]ガーデンセンター藤里店）を左折、[[勢田川]]に沿って歩くと左手の森の中に神社がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0217.html 田上大水神社（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0218.html 田上大水御前神社（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たのえおおみすしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[田上大水神社]] to [[Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 |画像= [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]藤里町字大丸679 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 50.2 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 26.7 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田上大水神社：小事神主
田上大水御前神社：宮子 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 丸山さん、車塚 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''田上大水神社'''（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。外宮の摂社16社のうち第5位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11 - 12ページ。本記事では田上大水神社と同じ社地にあり、外宮摂社のうち第6位である'''田上大水御前神社'''（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）についても記述する。 両社の[[祭神]]は、外宮[[神官]]家の祖先であり、[[父子]]であるとされる伊勢文化舎（2008）：38ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]藤里町に鎮座する。外宮宮域・[[高倉山 (伊勢市)|高倉山]]に鎮座する[[山末神社]]の南に位置する。周辺は[[宅地]]化が進んでいるが、かつては[[水田]]が広がり、神社[[境内]]の[[森林|森]]は現今より目立っていた。付近には宮崎氏神社跡や山宮祭場跡など[[度会氏]]にまつわる[[史跡]]がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：268ページ。鎮座地の[[丘]]は[[濠]]に囲まれ学研パブリッシング（2013）：59ページ、東西22[[メートル|m]]、南北43m、比高5.5mである。宮域[[面積]]は約1,133[[平方メートル|m2]]。 田上大水神社と田上大水御前神社の[[社殿]]は同じ[[玉垣]]の中に鎮座する。社殿正面に向って左側面に鎮座するのが田上大水御前神社である式内社研究会 編（1990）：270ページ。社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、田上大水神社は南向き、田上大水御前神社は東向きに建てられている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。周辺住民からは「丸山さん」、「車塚」と呼ばれ、親しまれる。社地は「車塚一号墳」という名の[[古墳]]であり、[[前方後円墳]]だとされる。ただし、古墳は原形をとどめていない。[[牛車|御所車]]に形が似ていることから「車塚」と呼ばれる。 社名が長いことや、[[草奈伎神社|大間国生神社]]が「大間社」と「国生社」の2所からなることから、「田上」と「大水」の2所からなるとする[[古書]]（『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』など）もあるが、これは誤りである式内社研究会 編（1990）：267ページ。 == 祭神 == 田上大水神社の祭神は小事神主（おごとかんぬし）。外宮の[[世襲]]神主であった度会氏の祖先であるとされる。『[[度会県|度会縣]]宮社検査録』などの[[明治]]期の文献では祭神を不詳とするものが多い。 田上大水御前神社の祭神は、小事神主の[[娘]]であり、[[斎宮|斎王]]に奉仕したとされる宮子（みやこ）。 社名から推測できるように、元は[[水神|水の神]]であったのではないかとされる。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された。また、『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。社名は『延喜式神名帳』では「田上大水神社」、『止由気宮儀式帳』では「田上神社」と記す。 [[中世]]の末には[[祭祀]]が行われなくなる式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ。[[権禰宜]]の出口延良（度会延良）が[[承応]]元年（[[1652年]]）に度会四門の氏人らとともに再建した。この後の[[大宮司]]・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）による他の摂社の再興事業は、田上大水神社の再興に大きな影響を与えた。こうした再興の経緯により、[[明治]]初期の神宮改正まで氏人によって社殿の造り替えが続けられた。 社殿は[[1923年]]（大正12年）3月に建て替えられている宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ。また[[1958年]]（昭和33年）12月にも建て替えが行われ、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[9月20日]]に大修繕が施された。 == 交通 == * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「庁舎前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩約3分（約300m）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北上、「県庁舎前」[[交差点]]を左折、約2分（約1[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 ;山末神社から * 神社の入り口から南下し、木材置き場の前の交差点を左折、その先の園芸店（[[クラギ|農業屋]]ガーデンセンター藤里店）を左折、[[勢田川]]に沿って歩くと左手の森の中に神社がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0217.html 田上大水神社（たのえおおみずじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0218.html 田上大水御前神社（たのえおおみずみまえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たのえおおみすしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references />

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/ Ise Jingu Official Website]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}

They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.

== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōō era|ja|承応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are located in {{ill|Fujisato Town|ja|藤里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|sacred grove|ja|森林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 {{ill|square meters|ja|平方メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as hereditary priests ({{ill|hereditary|ja|世襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the {{ill|Saio (Imperial Priestess)|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a {{ill|suijin|lt=water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.

Based on the definition of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927). The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|route bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the {{ill|Setagawa River|ja|勢田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
