revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saishi|qq=}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō era]] (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gon-negi (assistant priest)|qq=}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Nobuyoshi|qq=}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Kiyonaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kōtsū]] [[:en:Transit bus|route bus]]. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saishi|qq=}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō era]] (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gon-negi (assistant priest)|qq=}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Nobuyoshi|qq=}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Kiyonaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kōtsū]] [[:en:Transit bus|route bus]]. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}

== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>

== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 堀}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古本}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō era]] (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gon-negi (assistant priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Kiyonaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kōtsū]] [[:en:Transit bus|route bus]]. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 堀}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古本}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō era]] (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gon-negi (assistant priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Kiyonaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kōtsū]] [[:en:Transit bus|route bus]]. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ise Grand Shrine
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 堀}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古本}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==

Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = 田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社 (Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine & Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine) | image = [[File:Tanoe Omizu shrine & Tanoe Omizu Mimae shrine.jpg|270px|thumb|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine]] | location = Fujisato-chō, Ōmaru 679, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} | Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''': Kojin no Kanushi (小事神主) * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''': Miyako (宮子) | Established = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor) * Fifth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) * Sixth-Ranked [[Sessha and Massha|Sessha]] for Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine | Architectural Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri|神明造]] (Shinmei style) | Other Names = Maruyama-san (丸山さん), Kurumazuka (車塚) | Annual Festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|神嘗祭]] | Map = Japan Mie }}
'''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' (田上大水神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Jinja*) and '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' (田上大水御前神社, *Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Jinja*) are auxiliary shrines (*[[sessha]]*) affiliated with {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine or Gekū) of the prestigious [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[Ise, Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == Both shrines share the same precincts, with Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine Ranked fifth and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine Ranked sixth among the sixteen auxiliary shrines of Toyouke Daijingu.<ref name="uy1">宇治山田市役所 編 (1929): pp. 11–12.</ref>
== Deities Enshrined == * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine''' enshrines '''Kojin no Kanushi''' (小事神主), revered as an ancestral deity of the external shrine's priestly lineage. * '''Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine''' enshrines '''Miyako''' (宮子), considered the offspring of Kojin no Kanushi, emphasizing the familial connection between the two deities.<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 (2008): p. 38.</ref>
== History == Established before 804 CE (Enryaku 23), both shrines have long-standing associations with Ise Grand Shrine's ritual practices, particularly participating in the annual [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] harvest festival.
== Architecture == Both shrines are built in the traditional '''[[Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]]''' architectural style, characterized by simplicity and antiquity, reflecting the sacred nature of Ise's Shinto shrines.
== Other Names == The shrines are locally known as '''Maruyama-san''' (丸山さん) and '''Kurumazuka''' (車塚), hinting at historical landmarks or folklore tied to their origins.
== Overview == {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} are located in [[:en:Fujisato, Akita|Fujisato Town]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="ic"/>. They are situated to the south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}}, which stands within the outer precincts of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically near {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takakura (Ise)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although the surrounding area has become residential, it was once covered with expansive {{ill|rice paddies|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice paddies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田}}, and the [[:en:Forest|sacred grove]] of the shrine precinct was more prominent than it is today<ref name="ic"/>. Nearby, there are historical sites related to the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, such as the ruins of {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎氏神社跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Yamamiya Ritual Site|ja|山宮祭場跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamiya Ritual Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 268</ref>.
The mound where the shrines stand is surrounded by a {{ill|moat|ja|濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 堀}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59</ref>, measuring 22 [[:en:Metre|meters]] east-to-west, 43 meters north-to-south, with a relative height of 5.5 meters<ref name="sk8"/>. The shrine precinct covers an area of approximately 1,133 [[:en:Square metre|square meters]]<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Shrine Structures ==
The shrine buildings ({{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}}) of both Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine and Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine are enshrined within the same [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence)<ref name="gk"/>. Facing the shrines from the front, Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine is located on the '''left''' side<ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 270</ref>.
Both shrines are built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺}}. The main building of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine faces '''south''', while Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine faces '''east'''<ref name="sk0"/>. There are no {{ill|offertory boxes|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory boxes|qq=}} placed at the shrines. Locally, they are affectionately referred to as "'''Maruyama-san'''" and "'''Kurumazuka'''" (meaning "Car Mound")<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds are known as the site of an ancient Kofun called "{{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|ja|車塚一号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurumazuka Kofun No. 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]<ref name="gk"/>. However, the original shape of the Kofun has not been preserved<ref name="sk8"/>. The name "Kurumazuka" comes from its resemblance to an ancient {{ill|ox-drawn carriage|ja|御所車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ox-drawn carriage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛車}}<ref name="sk8"/>.
== Naming and Historical Notes ==
Due to the shrine’s long name, some {{ill|ancient texts|ja|古書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古本}}—such as the *Ise Nisho Daijingu Jinmei Hisho*—mistakenly interpret it as consisting of two separate shrines: "Tanoe" and "Ōmizu." This misunderstanding arises from comparisons with shrines like {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which consists of two distinct shrines: "Ōma Shrine" and "Kuniumi Shrine." However, in the case of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine, this interpretation is incorrect<ref name="sk7">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 267</ref>. == Deities Enshrined ==
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} is '''Ogoto Kannushi''' (小事神主)<ref name="ic"/>. He is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}, who served as hereditary priests ([[:en:Line of hereditary succession|hereditary]]) of the Outer Shrine (Gekū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
Many records from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]—such as the *Watarai Prefecture Shrine Inspection Record* (*度会縣宮社検査録*)—list the enshrined deity as unknown<ref name="sk7"/>.
The deity enshrined at {{ill|Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|ja|田上大水御前神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanoe Ōmizu Mimae Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田上大水神社}} is '''Miyako''' (宮子), who is said to have been the daughter of Ogoto Kannushi and served as an attendant to the [[:en:Saiō|Saio (Imperial Priestess)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
As suggested by the shrine’s name, it is believed that the original deity may have been a [[:en:Suijin|water god]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk8"/>.
== History ==
Based on the definition of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]'s {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrines)|ja|摂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}, the shrine was established before the compilation of the *{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}*, that is, prior to the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} (927). Additionally, as it is mentioned in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō* (*止由気宮儀式帳*)<ref name="sk8"/>, it is confirmed to have existed prior to the 23rd year of the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (804). In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the shrine is listed as "Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine" (田上大水神社), while in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, it is referred to as "Tanoe Shrine" (田上神社)<ref name="sk7"/>.
By the end of the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], rituals ({{ill|saishi|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}}) were no longer conducted at the shrine<ref name="sk9">式内社研究会 編（1990）：269ページ</ref>. In the 1st year of the [[:en:Jōō (Edo period)|Jōō era]] (1652), the shrine was rebuilt by the {{ill|gon-negi (assistant priest)|ja|権禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gon-negi (assistant priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職}} {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|度会延良|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Nobuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) together with members of the Watarai Four Gates clan<ref name="sk9"/>. The subsequent restoration projects of other sessha led by the {{ill|ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ōmiya-tsukasa (chief priest)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Kiyonaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Kiyonaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi no Kiyonaga) significantly influenced the reconstruction of Tanoe Ōmizu Shrine<ref name="sk9"/>. Due to these restoration efforts, the shrine buildings continued to be periodically rebuilt by local clans until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] Shrine Reformation<ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine buildings were reconstructed in March 1923 (Taishō 12)<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：12ページ</ref>. Another reconstruction took place in December 1958 (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out on September 20, 1979 (Shōwa 54)<ref name="sk0"/>.
== Access ==
* Approximately a 3-minute walk (about 300 meters) from the "Chōsha-mae" bus stop on the [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kōtsū]] [[:en:Transit bus|route bus]]. * About 2 minutes (approximately 1 km) from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. Head north on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road), turn left at the "Kenchōsha-mae" intersection. There is no parking available nearby.
; From {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazue Shrine|qq=}} * Head south from the shrine entrance, turn left at the intersection in front of the lumber storage area. Then, turn left again at the gardening center ({{ill|Kuragi|ja|クラギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragi|qq=}} Agricultural Store Fujisato Branch). Walk along the [[:en:Seta River (Mie)|Setagawa River]], and the shrine will be on the left within the forest<ref name="ic"/>.
== location == * '''Address:''' 679 Ōmaru, Fujisato-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan * '''coordinates:''' {{coord|34|28|50.2|N|136|42|26.7|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
== Related Shrines == * [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
